home.headline: Preset Forge
home.subtitle: Edit, share, and sync Valeton GP-200 presets live via USB MIDI.

footer.disclaimer: Preset Forge — Unofficial tool. Not affiliated with Valeton.

device.connectHint: Connect via USB MIDI to your Valeton GP-200. Load, edit, and send presets live to the device.
device.versionWarning: Only tested with FW 1.2 — other versions at your own risk
device.firmwareBannerText: This app was only tested with firmware 1.2. Your version ({version}) may cause issues.

help.title: Help
help.gettingStartedLoad: Load a .prst file by dragging it into the editor or clicking "Load preset from disk".
help.gettingStartedEdit: Click on an effect slot to adjust parameters. Toggle effects on or off with the LED button.
help.gettingStartedSave: Click "Save preset as file" to download your edited preset as a .prst file.
help.gettingStartedReorder: Drag and drop effect slots to reorder the signal chain.
help.usbMidiBrowser: Web MIDI is only supported in Chrome and Edge. Firefox and Safari are not compatible.
help.usbMidiConnect: Connect your GP-200 via USB and click "Connect GP-200". The browser will ask for permission to access the MIDI device.
help.usbMidiLive: Changes in the editor are sent to the device instantly: toggling effects, changing parameters, and reordering the signal chain.
help.usbMidiPullPush: Use "Load" to pull a preset from the device and "Save" to push it back.
help.galleryUpload: Sign in and upload presets to your profile. Publish them to the gallery so others can discover them.
help.galleryShare: Each preset gets a unique share link. You can reset the link at any time to revoke access.
help.galleryDownload: Browse the gallery for presets from other users and download them directly.
help.bankNavTabs: When you load a preset via USB, the entire bank (A/B/C/D) is loaded. Switch between the four slots using the tabs.
help.bankNavPrevNext: Use the arrow buttons on the left and right of the tabs to navigate to the previous or next bank.
help.shortcutsNone: There are currently no dedicated keyboard shortcuts. All features are accessible through the interface.
help.faqBrowserA: The editor works in all modern browsers. For USB MIDI connections, Chrome or Edge is required.
help.faqFirmwareA: The app was tested with firmware 1.2. Other versions may work but are not officially supported.
help.faqFormatA: The .prst format is the proprietary preset file format of the Valeton GP-200. Each file is exactly 1224 bytes and contains 11 effect slots with up to 15 parameters each.
help.faqOfflineA: The editor itself works in the browser, but gallery and profile features require an internet connection.
